docs.title: Critic
docs.text:
We also have a critic markup extension. Using it, we can showcase added and removed text.

We can also show unchangedchanged text, comments, and highlights.

docs.title: Amuzil Blog
docs.text: This is the blog of Amuzil. It is a work in progress. Please be patient.

docs.title: Hello world!
docs.text:
Welcome to our first blog post. In blog posts like these, we can also use all kinds of fancy Markdown features to write a nice blog.

Let's try a table:

Syntax Description Header Title Paragraph Text

docs.title: Skills

docs.title: Skills
docs.text: The Magus skill API uses several terms, such as forms, techniques, and modifiers.

docs.title: Forms
docs.text: A form is a fundamental type of movement or interaction with the environment. They are mainly used as building blocks for input. They are often applied to a specific target, which can be a chunk of bendable element or in some cases a creature, animal or plant.

docs.title: Arc
docs.text: Makes the target swirl around you. Useful for charging lightning or whips, or generating a protective field around you. A selected target is not required for this form, as shown by it being usable for lightning.

docs.title: Block
docs.text: Blocks from an attack by the target.

docs.title: Breathe
docs.text: Focuses energy within the user's chest. Useful for buff abilities or techniques such as ice breath.

docs.title: Burst
docs.text: Violently shoots out the target, like a shotgun. The release of compressed force. More powerful than blast, but less controlled.

docs.title: Grow
docs.text: Increases the size of the target. Although sometimes it might have the same effect as the focus modifier, they are not the same and can have varying effects.

docs.title: Lower
docs.text: Lowers the target.

docs.title: Pull
docs.text: Pulls the target.

docs.title: Push
docs.text: Pushes the target.

docs.title: Raise
docs.text: Lifts the target.

docs.title: Shrink
docs.text: Compresses the target, for example into a sphere.

docs.title: Strike
docs.text: Strikes the target, for example in the techniques air gust and fire blast. Can be modified for ranged, or left as is for melee.

docs.title: Twist
docs.text: Twists the target. For example, it rotates a projectile.

docs.title: Element specific forms
docs.text: Elements can have forms unique to them.

docs.title: Modifiers
docs.text: A modifier is a simple piece of data that can be added by executing a form in a certain way.

docs.title: Common modifiers
docs.text: Common modifiers can be unlocked by anyone in the skill tree.

docs.title: Focus
docs.text: Charges the technique by holding down the form button.

docs.title: Multi
docs.text: Multiplies the technique by repeating the form button.

docs.title: Directional
docs.text: Aims the technique in a certain direction by walking that way. Additionally, there is a difference between "lower" and "upper", such as with a fire kick or a block. By default, the direction is "upper", but it can be changed by holding down the sneak key.

docs.title: Target
docs.text: Aims the technique at a target by looking at them.

docs.title: Special modifiers
docs.text: Special modifiers can be earned through various means, included out-of-game activities such as supporting the projects. These special modifiers include VFX and flavour finishers.

docs.title: Example
docs.text: An earth wall has a directional modifier, which specifies in what direction the wall appears. Earth spear has a target modifier, which means that you need to specify a target the spear should aim for.

docs.title: Techniques
docs.text:
A technique is a specific set of forms. This is the basic “move”. It consumes chi and has some kind of effect.

For example, think of earth wall as a technique that creates a wall of earth in front of the caster, or earth spear as a technique that launches a spear of earth at a target.

Techniques can be unlocked and upgraded in the skill tree.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: Here are some examples of techniques from the Avatar mod:

docs.title: Earth Technique
docs.text:
• Earth Spear
  • Earth Wall

docs.title: Earth Spear
docs.text: Earth Spear is a technique that launches a spear of earth at a target.
